title: Find Files (Linux)
description: Search for files in a directory hierarchy based on names or types.
when_to_use: When you need to locate a specific file on a Linux file system during a CTF or initial enumeration.

flag_descriptions.-name: Search for files with a specific name

title: Search File Contents (Linux)
description: Print lines matching a pattern. Extremely useful for finding passwords in config files.
when_to_use: When searching for passwords, specific keywords, or parsing log files.

title: Change File Permissions (Linux)
description: Modify the read/write/execute permissions of a file (e.g., making a script executable).
when_to_use: When you upload an exploit or script to a target machine and need to make it executable.

optional_flags.description: Give full read/write/execute permissions to everyone (dangerous!)

title: List Local Users (Windows)
description: Enumerate the local users on a Windows machine using PowerShell.
when_to_use: During initial post-exploitation on a Windows host to see who has accounts.

title: System Information (Windows)
description: Displays detailed configuration information about a computer and its operating system.
when_to_use: To gather OS version, build number, and hotfix info to look for unpatched privilege escalation vulnerabilities.

title: Check IP Address (Linux)
description: Show / manipulate routing, network devices, interfaces and tunnels.
when_to_use: To find out what your own IP address is (e.g., your TryHackMe VPN IP for a reverse shell).

title: ICMP Echo Request (Ping)
description: Send ICMP ECHO_REQUEST to network hosts to check if they are online.
when_to_use: To verify network connectivity to a target machine.

title: Basic Nmap Scan
description: The most fundamental Nmap scan taught in THM. Scans the top 1,000 ports.
when_to_use: As the very first step in enumerating a target machine to see what services are running.

optional_flags.description: Attempt to determine the version of the services running on open ports
Run default Nmap scripts for vulnerability discovery
Scan all 65535 ports instead of the top 1000
Skip host discovery (treat the host as online)

title: Aggressive Nmap Scan
description: An aggressive Nmap scan that combines OS detection, version scanning, script scanning, and traceroute.
when_to_use: When you want to gather as much information as possible quickly and stealth is not a concern.

flag_descriptions.-A: Enable OS detection, version detection, script scanning, and traceroute

title: Web Directory Brute-Forcing
description: Use Gobuster to find hidden directories and files on a web server.
when_to_use: When you find a web server on a target and want to discover hidden admin panels or interesting files.

optional_flags.description: Also search for specific file extensions
Run with 50 concurrent threads to speed up the scan

title: Brute-Force SSH (Hydra)
description: Use Hydra to guess the password for an SSH service.
when_to_use: When you know a username on the target and SSH is open, and you want to try common passwords.

title: Start Metasploit Framework
description: Launch the Metasploit Framework console to exploit known vulnerabilities.
when_to_use: When you have discovered a vulnerability (e.g., EternalBlue, vsftpd backdoor) and want to use a pre-built exploit.

optional_flags.description: Quiet mode (don't print the startup banner)

title: Capture Packets (tcpdump)
description: A command-line packet analyzer. Used to intercept and display TCP/IP packets being transmitted.
when_to_use: During defensive exercises to monitor live network traffic for suspicious activity.

flag_descriptions.-i: Specify the network interface to listen on

optional_flags.description: Show the packet payload in both HEX and ASCII

title: Crack Passwords (John the Ripper)
description: Crack password hashes using a wordlist.
when_to_use: When you have dumped password hashes (e.g., /etc/shadow or NTLM hashes) and want to crack them offline.

optional_flags.description: Specify the hash format if John cannot auto-detect it

title: Crack Passwords (Hashcat)
description: Advanced password recovery utility. Often faster than John because it uses the GPU.
when_to_use: For offline password cracking when you need maximum performance.

flag_descriptions.-m: Hash type (e.g., 0 for MD5, 1000 for NTLM)
